feature: hex chat — Hooks, Dynamic Skills, and Command Execution
description: Implements ADR-2604011300 P7 (hooks) plus dynamic user skill loading (/skills, user-defined slash commands) and hex command execution (/hex <cmd>, hex_exec model tool). Brings hex chat to Claude Code parity for the session lifecycle and skill system.

done: hex-cli/src/tui/chat.rs — full streaming TUI with tool use loop (P1–P6 complete)
hex-cli/src/tui/mcp_client.rs — embedded MCP client, full 40+ tool surface
hex-cli/src/commands/hook.rs — SessionStart, SessionEnd, Route hooks exist in CLI

phases.name: Chat lifecycle hooks
phases.description: Fire existing hex hook events at the correct chat TUI lifecycle points. hex hook session-start / session-end / route already exist in hex-cli. This phase wires them into the TUI event loop.

tasks.name: Add run_hook() helper to chat.rs
tasks.description: Add async fn run_hook(event: &str) -> Option<String> to chat.rs. Spawns 'hex hook <event>' as a subprocess, captures stdout, returns trimmed output. Non-zero exit appends '(exit N)' to output. Never panics — returns None on spawn failure. Used by all hook call sites.

tasks.name: Fire session-start hook after TUI init
tasks.description: In chat.rs run() after enable_raw_mode() and terminal setup, call run_hook('session-start'). If output is non-empty, push a Role::Skill message at the top of the message list before the first draw. Satisfies S01.

tasks.name: Fire session-end hook on quit
tasks.description: In the event loop break path (q / Ctrl+C), call run_hook('session-end') before disable_raw_mode(). The session is already auto-saved; the hook runs after save. Satisfies S02.

tasks.name: Fire route hook before each inference turn
tasks.description: In send_message(), before spawning the stream_request task, call run_hook('route'). If the output contains a priority-2 inbox notification pattern (JSON with priority==2), push a Role::Skill message and do NOT spawn inference — set a pending_message field instead. User must /ack or Ctrl+A to proceed. This satisfies S03, S12. Also increment a notification_count: u32 on ChatApp; status bar shows '🔔 N' when > 0.

phases.name: Dynamic skill loading
phases.description: Load .claude/skills/*.md from project and global (~/.claude/skills/) directories. Make each skill available as a slash command. Add /skills to list them. Project overrides global on name collision.

tasks.name: Add skill loader to skills.rs
tasks.description: Add fn load_user_skills() -> Vec<(String, String)> to skills.rs. Returns (slash_name, markdown_body) pairs. Scan: (1) .claude/skills/*.md relative to CWD, (2) ~/.claude/skills/*.md. Filename stem becomes the slash command name (kebab-case preserved). Project files override global on name collision. Returns empty vec if no skills found — no error.

tasks.name: Load skills at ChatApp startup and store in state
tasks.description: In chat.rs run(), call load_user_skills() after MCP spawn. Store Vec<(String, String)> in ChatApp as user_skills field. This is read-only after load — no dynamic refresh needed.

tasks.name: Add /skills command and dynamic skill dispatch to dispatcher
tasks.description: In skills.rs dispatch(), before the match: (a) add '/skills' command that returns Lines listing all user_skills names + first non-empty line of each body as description. (b) After the built-in match falls through to _, check user_skills for a matching name. If found, return SkillResult::InjectMessage(body) — a new variant that tells ChatApp to put the skill markdown body into the input box (user can edit before sending) OR send directly. Pass user_skills: &[(String,String)] as a new parameter to dispatch(). Update all call sites. Satisfies S04, S05, S10.

phases.name: Hex command execution
phases.description: Add /hex <subcommand> slash command that runs hex CLI commands and streams output into the TUI. Add hex_exec as an MCP tool so the model can invoke hex commands directly.

tasks.name: Add /hex slash command with live output streaming
tasks.description: In skills.rs, add '/hex' command. Parse rest as argv (split on whitespace). Spawn hex binary (std::env::current_exe()) with those args, capture combined stdout+stderr line by line. Return SkillResult::Lines with all output lines. Truncate at 200 lines to avoid flooding the TUI. Never use sh -c — use Command::new(exe).args(argv). Satisfies S06, S11.

tasks.name: Add POST /api/exec endpoint to hex-nexus
tasks.description: Add POST /api/exec to hex-nexus axum router. Body: {"subcommand": "plan list"}. Splits subcommand on whitespace, prepends hex binary path, spawns process, captures output (stdout+stderr), returns {"output": "...", "exit_code": 0}. Timeout: 30s. Never shell-interpolates — argv only. Guards against empty subcommand. Satisfies S07, S11.

tasks.name: Add hex_exec tool schema to mcp-tools.json
tasks.description: Add hex_exec tool entry to hex-cli/assets/schemas/mcp-tools.json. Schema: {name: string 'hex_exec', description: 'Execute a hex CLI subcommand and return its output', inputSchema: {type: object, properties: {subcommand: {type: string, description: 'hex subcommand and args, e.g. "plan list" or "adr search auth"'}}, required: ['subcommand']}}. In mcp.rs dispatch_tool(), add hex_exec case that calls POST /api/exec. Satisfies S07.

phases.name: Status bar improvements and integration test
phases.description: Surface notification badge in status bar, update /help with new commands, verify end-to-end with cargo check.

tasks.name: Add notification badge to status bar
tasks.description: In render_status(), if app.notification_count > 0 append ' · 🔔 N unread' to the status string in Color::Yellow. Clear notification_count when user uses /inbox or types /ack. Satisfies S12.

tasks.name: Update /help with new slash commands
tasks.description: Update /help output in skills.rs dispatch to include: /skills (list available skills), /hex <cmd> (run hex command), and note that user skills are loaded from .claude/skills/. Keep existing 7 commands.

mergeOrder: P1 (hooks) and P2 (skills) and P3.1 (P3 primary) are independent — run in parallel
P3.2 (nexus exec endpoint) parallel with P1/P2
P4 after all P1–P3 tasks complete

riskRegister.risk: hex hook session-start is slow (registers agent, hits nexus) and blocks TUI startup
riskRegister.mitigation: Run hook in a background tokio task; push output to TUI via the existing token_tx channel rather than blocking

riskRegister.risk: Route hook fires on every user message — adds latency if nexus is slow
riskRegister.mitigation: 10s timeout on hook subprocess; if timeout, skip hook output and continue normally

riskRegister.risk: User skills with very large bodies (~100KB) injected into model context
riskRegister.mitigation: Truncate skill body to 8000 chars before injection; show truncation notice

successCriteria: hex chat starts and shows session-start hook output (agent registration, project context) before the input prompt
/hex plan list shows workplan output inside the TUI within 5 seconds
Model can call hex_exec tool and get hex adr list output back
Quitting with q fires session-end hook
cargo build -p hex-cli -p hex-nexus --release succeeds with no errors
